summary_text: **Review Memo: Quoted-Author–Driven Draft Generator (Notion Draft Only)** **Key Risks (ordered by severity)** 1. **Control-plane breach: draft system mutates publish state** - Failure mode: generated rows are accidentally marked `approved` or scheduled into live slots. - Impact: unintended posting behavior, slot contamination. - Context fit: violates current rule that Notion is authoritative and posting mode defaults to draft. 2. **Provenance failure and unverifiable claims** - Failure mode: candidate is based on an X post with missing source lineage (deleted post, partial thread, quote out of context). - Impact: low-trust content, factual drift, weak first-source posture. - Context fit: conflicts with your primary-source-first brand and `note-manuscript` discipline. 3. **Echo-chamber amplification** - Failure mode: historically quoted authors dominate future drafts, narrowing viewpoint. - Impact: repetitive worldview, audience fatigue, lower originality. - Context fit: clashes with your pattern rotation and “new angle/new evidence” requirement. 4. **Attribution/copyright and ethical reuse risk** - Failure mode: drafts paraphrase too closely, over-quote, or replicate thread structure. - Impact: reputational and platform-policy risk. - Context fit: especially risky when using same authors repeatedly. 5. **Stale-source drift** - Failure mode: candidate uses old/edited/deleted posts, or ignores updated primary docs. - Impact: outdated takes pushed into draft queue. - Context fit: undermines time-sensitive medical/policy commentary. 6. **Duplicate/redundancy leakage** - Failure mode: semantically same claim enters draft queue despite text-level differences. - Impact: queue bloat, repeated narratives, lower perceived quality. - Context fit: current `posted.json` checks are mostly string/signature oriented, not full claim novelty. 7. **Abuse/quality traps from engagement-biased retrieval** - Failure mode: system learns “high engagement” instead of “high evidence quality.” - Impact: hot-take drift, less clinical rigor. - Context fit: can erode the current high-signal positioning. --- **Must-Have Safeguards** 1. **Hard publication boundary** - Generator can only create Notion rows with `Status=draft`. - Must never set `approved`, `scheduled`, `posted`, `Tweet ID`, or publish metadata. - Enforce at write layer and schema validation layer. 2. **Notion-authoritative write contract** - All generated candidates must be written to Notion first. - Local/Supabase mirrors are read-only for this feature. - If conflict exists, Notion wins. 3. **Mandatory provenance schema per candidate** - Required fields: source post URL, author handle, post ID, retrieved_at, thread depth, extraction method, and confidence. - Missing provenance => candidate rejected. 4. **First-source gate before candidate acceptance** - Candidate must include at least one primary-source URL (or explicit `primary_source_missing=true` with auto-reject). - X-only commentary cannot pass to final draft list. 5. **Attribution guardrail** - No long verbatim excerpts; short quote cap and forced paraphrase. - Must include explicit “inspired by / triggered by” source tracking in internal metadata. - Keep proper nouns in `Source URLs`/metadata unless user explicitly wants body mention. 6. **Staleness and deletion checks** - Re-resolve source post before draft creation. - TTL gate (e.g., reject if post older than configured window unless “evergreen” tag). - Reject deleted/edited-without-refresh sources. 7. **Semantic dedupe gate** - Compare against Notion drafts + approved + posted history at claim/topic level, not only text hash. - Reject near-duplicate thesis even if wording differs. 8. **Diversity quota to prevent echo chamber** - Cap per-author draft share in rolling window. - Require topic/pattern/category spread aligned with current x-auto profile. 9. **Slot isolation** - This feature must not allocate scheduler slots. - It cannot consume or alter fixed slot policy (`06:50/11:50/16:...
